title: GENARCH – Launch
summary: Platform launch with expanded research database: genes, pathways, diseases, and environmental exposures from peer-reviewed biomedical and genomic studies. Community and Knowledge Graph modules are live. Follow us @genarch_offcial on Instagram.
content:
## GENARCH – Launch

This release marks the official launch of the GENARCH platform.

### Expanded datasets
- **Diseases**: Significantly expanded entries from peer-reviewed biomedical literature, with consistent schema and scientifically accurate terminology.
- **Genes**: Extended gene catalog with molecular function, expression context, and linked diseases and exposures.
- **Pathways**: Pathway entries with environmental triggers, key genes, and tissue specificity.
- **Environmental exposures**: Broadened exposure definitions, proxies, sensitive windows, and G×E highlights.

### Platform launch
- Atlas, Mechanism Briefs, Knowledge Graph, Community module, Methods, and Passport are live.
- Cross-linking across mechanisms, passport pages, and region-specific exposure pages is maintained.

### Connect with us
- Official Instagram: **@genarch_offcial**

All content is for educational purposes. Population-level estimates do not imply individual risk.

title: GENARCH v1.0 — Initial Atlas Release
summary: Initial release of the GENARCH atlas with seed content focused on asthma, PM2.5 air pollution, IL33 genetics, NF-κB pathway, and the PM2.5–IL33–NF-κB mechanism brief. Includes knowledge graph linking disease, exposure, gene, pathway, and tissue entities. Community module provides placeholder for Loudoun County, Virginia.
changes: Launched Atlas Core with Asthma disease page
Added PM2.5 exposure module with G×E highlights (IL33, GSTP1, TNF)
Implemented knowledge graph with nodes and edges (evidence_type, direction, tissue, strength, confidence, sources, year_first_reported, ancestry_rep)
Community module for Loudoun County, Virginia with health stats and exposure layers
Search index for fuzzy search across entities
content:
## GENARCH v1.0 — Initial Atlas Release

This release marks the first public availability of the GENARCH gene–environment atlas.

### Disease: Asthma
- Complete profile with genetic architecture (IL33, TSLP, ORMDL3/GSDMB, IL1RL1, RAD50)
- Real GWAS data (rsIDs, p-values, effect sizes)
- Heritability and PRS transferability notes
- Exposure modifiers: PM2.5, ozone, prenatal tobacco, endotoxin
- Tissues: bronchial epithelium, alveolar macrophages, mast cells, Th2 lymphocytes

### Exposure: Ambient Air Pollution (PM2.5)
- Definition, proxies, systems affected
- Sensitive windows: prenatal, early childhood, adolescence
- G×E: PM2.5 × IL33, GSTP1, TNF

### Gene: IL33
- Epithelial alarmin; asthma-associated eQTLs
- Pathways: NF-κB
- Linked diseases and exposures

### Pathway: NF-κB
- KEGG hsa04064
- Environmental triggers, key genes
- Linked to asthma and air pollution

### Knowledge Graph
- 8 nodes, 12 edges
- Edge attributes: evidence_type, direction, tissue, strength, confidence, sources, year_first_reported, ancestry_rep

### Community: Loudoun County, VA
- Asthma hospitalization and prevalence stats
- Exposure layers: PM2.5, industrial proximity, food access
- Hotspot model with SHAP summaries

### Data Sources
- GWAS Catalog, GTEx, EPA AQS, Virginia Department of Health, scientific literature with DOIs
